response:
**Why Does My Business Need a Website?**

Hey there. Let’s be real — starting a business is exciting. You’ve got your idea, your passion, and maybe even a few customers already. But now you’re thinking, *“Why does my business need a website?”* That’s a great question. I mean, you’ve got your Instagram page, your Facebook group, and maybe even a few flyers handed out at the local coffee shop. So why does a website even matter? Let’s break it down — not just for the sake of checking a box, but because it could be one of the most important decisions you make for your business growth.

First off, a website is like your 24/7 storefront. Think about it — when someone is looking for a product or service, they’re probably on their phone or computer. They might not be near your physical location, and they might not even know you exist yet. A website gives you that digital presence — it’s your way of being available to people, anytime, anywhere.

But it’s not just about visibility. It’s also about credibility. In today’s world, people tend to trust businesses that have a professional online presence. If you’re running a local bakery, and someone Googles “best cakes in town,” they’re going to look for a website. If you don’t have one, they might assume you’re not serious or that you’re too small to be worth their time. A website, on the other hand, shows that you’re organized, professional, and ready to do business.

Now, let’s talk about the people who might find your website. You might be targeting local customers, or maybe you want to reach an international audience. Either way, a website is your way of reaching them. It’s your digital business card. It’s where people can learn more about your products, see your services, and even contact you directly.

And here’s the thing — a website is also a great place to tell your story. You can share your mission, your values, and even your journey. That kind of connection is powerful. It helps people see you as more than just a business — it helps them see you as a person, someone who cares about what they do.

But I know what you’re thinking — “Okay, that all sounds great, but how do I even start?” That’s a valid question. Building a website might feel overwhelming, especially if you’re not tech-savvy. You might be wondering what platform to
